V-M-066.purpose: Verify Phase-48 .conf download MIME hardening across backend headers, frontend Blob creation, and protected operational surfaces.
V-M-066.checks: application/octet-stream attachment response, filename and filename-star .conf metadata, nosniff, no-store/private cache control, byte-preserved config body, frontend Blob MIME alignment, auth preservation, and no deploy/install changes.

commands.command: cd backend && SECRET_KEY=test-secret-key-with-enough-length PYTHONPATH=. ../.venv/bin/python -m pytest -o addopts='' tests/test_vpn_router_compat.py -v

python3 -m xml.etree.ElementTree docs/plans/Phase-48.xml docs/modules/M-066.xml docs/verification/V-M-066.xml docs/graph-index.xml docs/plan-index.xml docs/verification-index.xml docs/current-status.xml

module-checks.check-1: /api/v1/vpn/config/download returns Content-Type application/octet-stream and never text/plain for attachment downloads.
module-checks.check-2: Content-Disposition contains attachment, filename="*.conf", and filename*=UTF-8''*.conf with no .txt suffix.
module-checks.check-3: X-Content-Type-Options is nosniff and Cache-Control is private/no-store so browsers do not reinterpret the payload as text.
module-checks.check-4: Config body remains equivalent to the generated WireGuard/Amnezia config text after UTF-8 encoding.
module-checks.check-5: Frontend download Blob creation uses application/octet-stream and the download attribute ends in .conf for all config download branches.
module-checks.check-6: Pending-trial first config access, QR display, raw config copy, and device provisioning API compatibility remain intact.
module-checks.check-7: Smoke fails if Phase-48 changes install.sh, deploy scripts, nginx, docker-compose, TLS material, or VPN profile generation logic.
module-checks.check-8: Filename sanitation rejects path separators, duplicate .conf/.txt suffixes, control characters, non-ASCII surprises, and empty names before headers are built.
module-checks.check-9: Backend and frontend tests never print private keys, full config bodies, Authorization headers, tokens, or generated QR payloads.

verification-levels.module-local: Backend tests cover headers, filename sanitation, auth preservation, body byte preservation, pending-trial first-config access, and no regression in JSON/QR/raw config routes.
verification-levels.wave-level: Frontend build and static smoke cover Blob MIME, download attribute names, object URL cleanup, no .conf.txt fallback, and unchanged user-facing config workflow.
verification-levels.phase-level: XML parse, MyGRACE lint, and protected deploy/install/nginx/docker-compose diff guard prove documentation consistency and operational isolation.
verification-levels.live-optional: Manual Android Chrome/iOS Safari download proof is useful after prerelease deploy, but local execution can pass with deterministic HTTP/header and frontend static evidence.

scenarios.scenario-1: Android Chrome downloads krotpn-user.conf from the backend endpoint and does not receive text/plain metadata that would invite .txt renaming.
scenarios.scenario-2: A frontend-managed device config download creates an octet-stream Blob and suggests krotpn-device.conf.
scenarios.scenario-3: A verified pending-trial user can still retrieve the first VPN config, with only attachment metadata changed.
scenarios.scenario-4: QR, copy-to-clipboard, raw config display, and JSON config retrieval stay text-friendly while only the attachment download response is octet-stream.
scenarios.scenario-5: Any response header, filename helper, or frontend branch emits .conf.txt, text/plain, unsafe path separators, duplicate suffixes, or missing nosniff.
scenarios.scenario-6: Any Phase-48 diff touches deploy/install/nginx/docker-compose/TLS files or changes generated config content.

telemetry-requirements.requirement-1: Runtime telemetry is not required for Phase-48 because the change is deterministic HTTP metadata and frontend Blob construction, not VPN tunnel behavior.
telemetry-requirements.requirement-2: Backend logs may include secret-free filename policy decisions, route name, response MIME, and safe failure code; they must not include config body, private key, preshared key, Authorization header, access/refresh token, or QR payload.
telemetry-requirements.requirement-3: Smoke output must be aggregate marker-only evidence and must not print generated configs, object URLs containing secrets, or live user identifiers.

redaction-rules.rule-1: Do not log raw private keys, full config bodies, access tokens, refresh tokens, email tokens, MTProto secrets, or deployment secrets in tests or smoke output.

V-M-066.autonomous-execution-safety: Allowed after plan and verification approval because Phase-48 is restricted to response metadata, frontend Blob type, tests, and static smoke while deployment and VPN profile generation remain out of scope.
